taxonomy: Cuspidaria pterocarpa is classified as a species within the family Mytilidae, which is a family of marine bivalve mollusks. Mytilidae is part of the order Mytilida, which consists of marine bivalves with hinge teeth on the right valve. The order Mytilida is further classified under the class Bivalvia and the phylum Mollusca. The class Bivalvia is a group of marine and freshwater mollusks characterized by a two-chambered shell. This classification reflects the complex relationships and characteristics of Cuspidaria pterocarpa within the broader context of molluskan evolution.
